gold_answer: Yoga is the restraining of the mind-stuff from taking its various forms or modifications.
question: How is yoga itself defined in terms of the mind-stuff (chitta) and its modifications (vrittis)?

hi.gold_answer: योग मन-सामग्री को उसके विभिन्न रूपों या संशोधनों को लेने से रोकना है।
hi.question: योग को स्वयं मन-वस्तु (चित्त) और उसके संशोधनों (वृत्ति) के संदर्भ में कैसे परिभाषित किया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: योगः मनस्य विविधरूपाणां वा परिवर्तनानां वा ग्रहणात् निरोधः अस्ति।
sa.question: योगः स्वयमेव मनस्य (चित्तः) तथा तस्य परिवर्तनानां (वृत्तिः) दृष्ट्या कथं परिभाषितः अस्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: Yoga is restraining the mind-stuff (Chitta) from taking various forms (Vrittis).

_nearmiss_src.text_en: At that time (After all waves have finished. This is nothing to take with concentration)the seer (Purusha) rests in his own (unmodified) state.
There are five classes of modifications, (some) painful and (others) not painful.

gold_answer: The five obstructions are ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life.
question: What are the five pain-bearing obstructions (kleshas) that are named together?

hi.gold_answer: पाँच बाधाएँ अज्ञानता, अहंकार, आसक्ति, घृणा और जीवन से चिपके रहना हैं।
hi.question: पाँच दर्द-वाहक बाधाएँ (क्लेशा) क्या हैं जिन्हें एक साथ नामित किया गया है?

sa.gold_answer: अज्ञानः, अहंभावः, आसक्तिः, द्वेषः, जीवने आसक्तः च पञ्च बाधकाः सन्ति।
sa.question: पञ्च वेदनायुक्ताः बाधकाः (क्लेशा) काः सन्ति ये मिलित्वा नामाङ्किताः सन्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: The pain-bearing obstructions are — ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Ignorance is the productive field of all these that follow, whether they are dormant, attenuated, overpowered, or expanded.

The obstructions to Yoga are killing, falsehood, etc., whether committed, caused, or approved; either through avarice, or anger, or ignorance; whether slight, middling, or great; and they result in infinite ignorance and misery. This is (the method of) thinking the contrary.
_nearmiss_src.translit: vitarkā hiṃsādayaḥ kṛtakāritānumoditā lobhakrodhamohapūrvakā mṛdumadhyādhimātrā duḥkhājñānānantaphalā iti pratipakṣabhāvanam

gold_answer: The eight limbs are Yama, Niyama, Asana, Pranayama, Pratyahara, Dharana, Dhyana, and Samadhi.
question: What are the eight limbs of yoga, enumerated in order?

hi.gold_answer: आठ अंग हैं यम, नियम, आसन, प्राणायाम, प्रत्याहार, धारणा, ध्यान और समाधि।
hi.question: योग के आठ अंग कौन से हैं, जिन्हें क्रम में गिना गया है?

sa.gold_answer: अष्टौ अङ्गौ यमः, नियमः, आसनः, प्राणायामः, प्रत्याहारः, धारणा, ध्यानः, समाधिः च सन्ति।
sa.question: योगस्य अष्टांगाणि काः क्रमानुसारे गणिताः सन्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: Yama, Niyama, Āsana, Prānāyāma, Pratyāhāra, Dhāranā, Dhyāna, and Samādhi are the eight limbs of Yoga.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Non-killing, truthfulness, non-stealing, continence, and non-receiving are called Yamas.
Internal and external purification, contentment, mortification, study, and worship of God are the Niyamas.

gold_answer: The Yamas are non-killing, truthfulness, non-stealing, continence, and non-receiving.
question: What are the five restraints called the Yamas?

hi.gold_answer: यम गैर-हत्या, सच्चाई, गैर-चोरी, महाद्वीप और गैर-प्राप्त करने वाले हैं।
hi.question: पाँच प्रतिबंधों को यम कहा जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: यमजनाः अ-हत्याः, सत्यनिष्ठाः, अ-चोरीः, महाद्वीपाः, न ग्रहीताः च सन्ति।
sa.question: यम इति नाम्ना ख्याताः पञ्च प्रतिबन्धकाः काः सन्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: Non-killing, truthfulness, non-stealing, continence, and non-receiving are called Yamas.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Internal and external purification, contentment, mortification, study, and worship of God are the Niyamas.
Yama, Niyama, Āsana, Prānāyāma, Pratyāhāra, Dhāranā, Dhyāna, and Samādhi are the eight limbs of Yoga.

gold_answer: The Niyamas are internal and external purification, contentment, mortification, study, and worship of God.
question: What are the observances called the Niyamas?

hi.gold_answer: नियम आंतरिक और बाहरी शुद्धिकरण, संतुष्टि, मृत्यु, अध्ययन और भगवान की पूजा हैं।
hi.question: इन अनुष्ठानों को नियम क्या कहा जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: नियमः इति आन्तरिकं बाह्यं च शुद्दीकरणं, संतृप्तिः, मृत्युः, अध्ययनम्, ईश्वरस्य पूजा च सन्ति।
sa.question: नियमाः इति काः आचरणाः उच्यन्ते?

_gold_src.text_en: Internal and external purification, contentment, mortification, study, and worship of God are the Niyamas.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Non-killing, truthfulness, non-stealing, continence, and non-receiving are called Yamas.
Yama, Niyama, Āsana, Prānāyāma, Pratyāhāra, Dhāranā, Dhyāna, and Samādhi are the eight limbs of Yoga.

gold_answer: The manifesting word of Ishvara is Om.
question: What is said to be the manifesting word of Ishvara?

hi.gold_answer: ईश्वर का प्रकट शब्द ओम है।
hi.question: ईश्वर का प्रकट शब्द क्या कहा जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: ईश्वरस्य प्रकटः शब्दः ओम् अस्ति।
sa.question: ईश्वरस्य प्रकटः शब्दः कः इति कथ्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: His manifesting word is Om.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Or by devotion to Ishvara.
The repetition of this (Om) and meditating on its meaning (is the way).

gold_answer: Dharana is holding the mind fixed on some particular object.
question: How is Dharana, the holding of the mind, defined?

hi.gold_answer: धारणा मन को किसी विशेष वस्तु पर स्थिर कर रही है।
hi.question: धारणा, मन की पकड़, को कैसे परिभाषित किया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: धारणा मनस्य कस्मिंश्चित् विशिष्टे विषये स्थिरीकरणं करोति।
sa.question: मनसि धारणं कथं परिभाषितम्?

_gold_src.text_en: Dhāranā is holding the mind on to some particular object.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: An unbroken flow of knowledge in that object is Dhyāna.
When that, giving up all forms, reflects only the meaning, it is Samādhi.

gold_answer: Dhyana is an unbroken flow of knowledge directed toward that one object.
question: How is Dhyana (meditation) defined?

hi.gold_answer: ध्यान उस एक उद्देश्य की ओर निर्देशित ज्ञान का एक अखंड प्रवाह है।
hi.question: ध्यान (ध्यान) को कैसे परिभाषित किया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: ध्यानः एकः अविच्छिन्नः ज्ञानप्रवाहः अस्ति यः एकस्यां वस्तुं प्रति निर्देशितः अस्ति।
sa.question: ध्यानस्य (ध्यानस्य) परिभाषा कथं भवति?

_gold_src.text_en: An unbroken flow of knowledge in that object is Dhyāna.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Dhāranā is holding the mind on to some particular object.
When that, giving up all forms, reflects only the meaning, it is Samādhi.

gold_answer: When the three are practised together upon one object it is called Samyama.
question: What is the name given to the three practices of holding, meditation, and absorption when applied together to a single object?

hi.gold_answer: जब तीनों का एक साथ एक ही वस्तु पर अभ्यास किया जाता है तो इसे समय कहा जाता है।
hi.question: धारण, ध्यान और अवशोषण की तीन प्रथाओं को क्या नाम दिया गया है जब एक ही वस्तु पर एक साथ लागू किया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: एकस्मिन् विषये एकत्र तिस्रः अभ्यासः भवति चेत् तत् समयम् इति कथ्यते।
sa.question: धारणस्य, ध्यानस्य, अवशोषणस्य च त्रिभिः अभ्यासानां नाम किम्, यदा एकस्यां वस्तुं सह प्रयुक्तं भवति?

_gold_src.text_en: (These) three (when practised) in regard to one object is Samyama.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: When that, giving up all forms, reflects only the meaning, it is Samādhi.
By the conquest of that comes light of knowledge.

gold_answer: Ignorance is taking the non-eternal, impure, painful, and non-Self for the eternal, pure, happy, and the Self.
question: How is ignorance (avidya) defined with respect to the eternal and the Self?

hi.gold_answer: अज्ञान अनन्त, अशुद्ध, दर्दनाक और आत्महीन को शाश्वत, शुद्ध, सुखी और आत्मा के लिए ले जा रहा है।
hi.question: शाश्वत और आत्मा के संबंध में अज्ञान (अविद्या) को कैसे परिभाषित किया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: अज्ञानं नित्यम्, अशुद्धं, पीडितं, आत्मानं च शाश्वतं, शुद्धं, प्रसन्नं, आत्मानं च ग्रहीयते।
sa.question: शाश्वतस्य आत्मस्य च विषये अज्ञानस्य (अविद्या) परिभाषा कथं भवति?

_gold_src.text_en: Ignorance is taking the non-eternal, the impure, the painful, and the non-Self for the eternal, the pure, the happy, and the Atman or Self (respectively).

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Ignorance is the productive field of all these that follow, whether they are dormant, attenuated, overpowered, or expanded.
The pain-bearing obstructions are — ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life.

gold_answer: Cultivating friendship, mercy, gladness, and indifference toward those respective objects pacifies the mind.
question: Which four attitudes, directed toward the happy, unhappy, good, and evil, are said to pacify the mind?

hi.gold_answer: उन संबंधित वस्तुओं के प्रति मित्रता, दया, खुशी और उदासीनता विकसित करने से मन शांत होता है।
hi.question: कहा जाता है कि कौन से चार दृष्टिकोण, जो खुश, दुखी, अच्छे और बुरे के प्रति निर्देशित होते हैं, मन को शांत करते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: मित्रतायाः, दयायाः, आनन्दस्य, तेषां विषये उदासीनतायाः च संवर्धनेन मनस्य प्रशान्तिः भवति।
sa.question: सुखम्, दुःखम्, शुभं, अशुभं च प्रति निर्देशिताः काः चत्वारः मनोवृत्तयः मनस्य शान्तिं कुर्वन्ति इति कथ्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: Friendship, mercy, gladness, and indifference, being thought of in regard to subjects, happy, unhappy, good, and evil respectively, pacify the Chitta.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: To remedy this, the practice of one subject (should be made).
By throwing out and restraining the Breath.

gold_answer: Posture is that which is firm and pleasant.
question: What are the two qualities by which posture (asana) is characterized?

hi.gold_answer: मुद्रा वह है जो दृढ़ और सुखद हो।
hi.question: वे कौन से दो गुण हैं जिनके द्वारा आसन की विशेषता है?

sa.gold_answer: मुद्रा दृढं सुखप्रदं च भवति।
sa.question: कानि द्वौ गुणौ आसनस्य (आसनस्य) लक्षणौ स्तः?

_gold_src.text_en: Posture is that which is firm and pleasant.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By lessening the natural tendency (for restlessness) and meditating on the unlimited, posture becomes firm and pleasant.
Seat being conquered, the dualities do not obstruct.

gold_answer: The control of the modifications is achieved through practice and non-attachment.
question: Which aphorism states that the modifications of the mind are controlled by practice and non-attachment?

hi.gold_answer: संशोधनों का नियंत्रण अभ्यास और अनासक्ति के माध्यम से प्राप्त किया जाता है।
hi.question: किस सूत्र में कहा गया है कि मन के परिवर्तन अभ्यास और अनासक्ति द्वारा नियंत्रित होते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: परिवर्तनानां नियन्त्रणं अभ्यासेन अनासक्त्या च साध्यते।
sa.question: मनस्य परिवर्तनं अभ्यासेन अनासक्त्या च नियन्त्र्यते इति कः सूत्रः वदति?

_gold_src.text_en: Their control is by practice and non-attachment.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Continuous struggle to keep them (the Vrittis) perfectly restrained is practice.
That effect which comes to those who have given up their thirst after objects, either seen or heard, and which wills to control the objects, is non-attachment.

gold_answer: Disease, laziness, doubt, lack of enthusiasm, lethargy, sense-craving, false perception, failing to reach concentration, and falling away from it are the obstructing distractions.
question: Where are the obstacles to concentration, such as disease, doubt, and lethargy, listed?

hi.gold_answer: रोग, आलस्य, संदेह, उत्साह की कमी, सुस्ती, इन्द्रिय-लालसा, झूठी धारणा, एकाग्रता तक पहुँचने में विफलता, और इससे दूर जाना, विचलित करने वाली बाधाएँ हैं।
hi.question: एकाग्रता की बाधाएँ, जैसे रोग, संदेह और सुस्ती, कहाँ सूचीबद्ध हैं?

sa.gold_answer: रोगः, आलस्यः, सन्देहः, उत्साहस्य अभावः, आलस्यः, इन्द्रिय-तृषा, मिथ्या-बोधः, एकाग्रतां प्राप्तुं विफलाः, ततः दूरं गच्छन्तः च बाधकाः विचलनाः सन्ति।
sa.question: एकाग्रतायाः बाधायाः सूची कुत्र अस्ति, यथा रोगः, सन्देहः, आलस्यः च?

_gold_src.text_en: Disease, mental laziness, doubt, lack of enthusiasm, lethargy, clinging to sense-enjoyments, false perception, non-attaining concentration, and falling away from the state when obtained, are the obstructing distractions.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Grief, mental distress, tremor of the body, irregular breathing, accompany non-retention of concentration.
From that is gained (the knowledge of) introspection, and the destruction of obstacles.

gold_answer: Kriya-Yoga consists of mortification, study, and surrendering the fruits of one's work to God.
question: Which passage defines Kriya-Yoga as mortification, study, and surrendering the fruits of work to God?

hi.gold_answer: क्रिया-योग में मृत्यु, अध्ययन और अपने कार्य के फल को भगवान को समर्पित करना शामिल है।
hi.question: कौन सा परिच्छेद क्रिया-योग को मृत्यु, अध्ययन और कार्य के फल को भगवान को समर्पित करने के रूप में परिभाषित करता है?

sa.gold_answer: क्रियायोगे आत्मत्यागः, अध्ययनम्, स्वकर्मफलानां समर्पणं च भवति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् खण्डे क्रियायोगस्य परिभाषा अस्ति यत् मृत्युः, अध्ययनम्, कर्मफलानि ईश्वराय समर्पणं च इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Mortification, study, and surrendering fruits of work to God are called Kriyā-Yoga.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: (It is for) the practice of Samadhi and minimising the pain-bearing obstructions.
Internal and external purification, contentment, mortification, study, and worship of God are the Niyamas.

gold_answer: When non-killing is firmly established, hostilities cease in the practitioner's presence.
question: Which passage states that when non-killing is established, all enmities cease in the presence of the practitioner?

hi.gold_answer: जब गैर-हत्या दृढ़ता से स्थापित हो जाती है, तो अभ्यासी की उपस्थिति में शत्रुता समाप्त हो जाती है।
hi.question: किस परिच्छेद में कहा गया है कि जब गैर-हत्या स्थापित हो जाती है, तो अभ्यासी की उपस्थिति में सभी शत्रुता समाप्त हो जाती है?

sa.gold_answer: यदा अहिंसायाः दृढतया स्थापना भवति तदा अभ्यासिनः समक्षं वैरं समाप्तं भवति।
sa.question: कः खण्डः कथयति यत् यदा नरहत्यां न भवति तदा अभ्यासिनः समक्षं सर्वाः शत्रुताः समाप्ताः भवन्ति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Non-killing being established, in his presence all enmities cease (in others).

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By the establishment of truthfulness the Yogi gets the power of attaining for himself and others the fruits of work without the works.
By the establishment of non-stealing all wealth comes to the Yogi.

gold_answer: By the establishment of non-stealing, all wealth comes to the yogi.
question: Where is it said that when non-stealing is established, all wealth comes to the yogi?

hi.gold_answer: चोरी न करने की स्थापना से, सारा धन योगी के पास आता है।
hi.question: यह कहाँ कहा जाता है कि जब चोरी न करना स्थापित हो जाता है, तो सारा धन योगी के पास आता है?

sa.gold_answer: अ-हरणस्य स्थापनया सर्वं धनं योगीम् आगच्छति।
sa.question: कुत्र कथितम् अस्ति यत् यदा चोर्यं न भवति तदा सर्वं धनं योगीम् आगच्छति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: By the establishment of non-stealing all wealth comes to the Yogi.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Non-killing being established, in his presence all enmities cease (in others).
By the establishment of continence energy is gained.

gold_answer: By the establishment of continence, energy is gained.
question: Which aphorism states that energy is gained by the establishment of continence?

hi.gold_answer: महाद्वीप की स्थापना से ऊर्जा प्राप्त होती है।
hi.question: किस सूत्र में कहा गया है कि ऊर्जा महाद्वीप की स्थापना से प्राप्त होती है?

sa.gold_answer: महाद्वीपस्य स्थापनेन शक्तिः प्राप्यते।
sa.question: किं सूत्रं वदति यत् महाद्वीपस्य स्थापनेन शक्तिः प्राप्यते इति?

_gold_src.text_en: By the establishment of continence energy is gained.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By the establishment of non-stealing all wealth comes to the Yogi.
When he is fixed in non-receiving, he gets the memory of past life.

gold_answer: By making samyama on the three sorts of change comes knowledge of the past and the future.
question: Which passage says that knowledge of the past and future arises from samyama on the three kinds of change?

hi.gold_answer: तीन प्रकार के परिवर्तनों पर संयम करने से अतीत और भविष्य का ज्ञान प्राप्त होता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि अतीत और भविष्य का ज्ञान तीन प्रकार के परिवर्तनों पर संयम से उत्पन्न होता है?

sa.gold_answer: त्रयाणां परिवर्तनानां विषये संयमं कृत्वा भूतकालस्य भविष्यकालस्य च ज्ञानं प्राप्यते।
sa.question: किं खण्डं वदति यत् अतीतस्य भविष्यस्य च ज्ञानं त्रयाणां परिवर्तनानां विषये साम्यात् उद्भवति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: By making Samyama on the three sorts of changes comes the knowledge of past and future.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By making Samyama on word, meaning and knowledge, which are ordinarily confused, comes the knowledge of all animal sounds.
By perceiving the impressions, (comes) the knowledge of past life.

gold_answer: By making samyama on the sun comes knowledge of the world.
question: Where is it said that samyama on the sun yields knowledge of the world?

hi.gold_answer: सूर्य पर संयम करने से संसार का ज्ञान प्राप्त होता है।
hi.question: यह कहाँ कहा जाता है कि सूर्य पर संयम से संसार का ज्ञान प्राप्त होता है?

sa.gold_answer: सूर्यस्य उपरि संयमं कृत्वा जगतः ज्ञानं प्राप्यते।
sa.question: सूर्यस्य उपरि संयमः विश्वस्य ज्ञानं जनयति इति कुत्र कथ्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: By making Samyama on the sun, (comes) the knowledge of the world.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On the moon, (comes) the knowledge of the cluster of stars.
On the pole-star, (comes) the knowledge of the motions of the stars.

gold_answer: By samyama on the navel circle comes knowledge of the constitution of the body.
question: Which passage states that samyama on the navel circle gives knowledge of the constitution of the body?

hi.gold_answer: नाभि वृत्त पर संयम से शरीर की संरचना का ज्ञान प्राप्त होता है।
hi.question: किस परिच्छेद में कहा गया है कि नाभि वृत्त पर संयम शरीर की संरचना का ज्ञान देता है?

sa.gold_answer: नाभि-वृत्तस्य संयमेन शरीरस्य संविधानस्य ज्ञानं प्राप्यते।
sa.question: के खण्डे लिखितम् अस्ति यत् नाभि-वृत्तस्य उपरि स्थितः संयमः शरीरस्य संरचनायाः ज्ञानं ददति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: On the navel circle, (comes) the knowledge of the constitution of the body.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On the hollow of the throat, (comes) cessation of hunger.
On the pole-star, (comes) the knowledge of the motions of the stars.

gold_answer: By samyama on the hollow of the throat comes cessation of hunger.
question: Where is it said that concentrating on the hollow of the throat brings the cessation of hunger and thirst?

hi.gold_answer: गले के खोखले हिस्से पर संयम से भूख की समाप्ति होती है।
hi.question: यह कहाँ कहा जाता है कि गले के खोखले हिस्से पर ध्यान केंद्रित करने से भूख और प्यास बंद हो जाती है?

sa.gold_answer: संयमेन कण्ठस्य छिद्रस्य उपरि क्षुधा समाप्ता भवति।
sa.question: कण्ठस्य छिद्रस्य उपरि ध्यानं स्थापयित्वा क्षुधा पिपासाः च समाप्ताः भवन्ति इति कुत्र कथ्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: On the hollow of the throat, (comes) cessation of hunger.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On the navel circle, (comes) the knowledge of the constitution of the body.
On the nerve called Kurma, (comes) fixity of the body.

gold_answer: By samyama on the form of the body, obstructing its perceptibility, the yogi's body becomes unseen.
question: Which passage explains how the yogi's body can become invisible?

hi.gold_answer: शरीर के रूप में संयम द्वारा, इसकी बोधगम्यता में बाधा डालते हुए, योगी का शरीर अदृश्य हो जाता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश बताता है कि योगी का शरीर कैसे अदृश्य हो सकता है?

sa.gold_answer: शरीरस्वरूपे संयमेन, तस्य ग्रहणक्षमतां बाधयन्, योगिनः शरीरम् अदृश्यं भवति।
sa.question: योगिनः शरीरं कथं अदृश्यं भवितुम् अर्हति इति कः खण्डः वर्णयति?

_gold_src.text_en: By making Samyama on the form of the body, the perceptibility of the form being obstructed and the power of manifestation in the eye being separated, the Yogi's body becomes unseen.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Karma is of two kinds — soon to be fructified and late to be fructified. By making Samyama on these, or by the signs called Arishta, portents, the Yogis know the exact time of separation from their bodies.
_nearmiss_src.translit: sopakramaṃ nirupakramaṃ ca karma tatsaṃyamād aparāntajñānam ariṣṭebhyo vā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: But not its contents, that not being the object of the Samyama.

gold_answer: By samyama on the two kinds of karma, or on the portents called Arishta, the yogi knows the exact time of death.
question: Where is it said that the yogi can know the exact time of departure from the body, including through portents?

hi.gold_answer: दो प्रकार के कर्मों पर संयम से, या अरिष्ट नामक संकेतों पर, योगी को मृत्यु का सही समय पता होता है।
hi.question: यह कहाँ कहा जाता है कि योगी शरीर से प्रस्थान के सही समय को जान सकता है, जिसमें संकेत भी शामिल हैं?

sa.gold_answer: द्वयोः प्रकारयोः कर्मयोः, अथवा अरिष्ट-नामकयोः लक्षणयोः, संयमेन, योगी मृत्योः निश्चितं समयं जानाति।
sa.question: योगिनः शरीरात् निर्गमनस्य यथार्थं समयं ज्ञातुं शक्नोति इति कुत्र कथ्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: Karma is of two kinds — soon to be fructified and late to be fructified. By making Samyama on these, or by the signs called Arishta, portents, the Yogis know the exact time of separation from their bodies.
_gold_src.translit: sopakramaṃ nirupakramaṃ ca karma tatsaṃyamād aparāntajñānam ariṣṭebhyo vā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By making Samyama on the form of the body, the perceptibility of the form being obstructed and the power of manifestation in the eye being separated, the Yogi's body becomes unseen.
By making Samyama on friendship, mercy, etc. (I.33), the Yogi excels in the respective qualities.

gold_answer: Non-attachment is the controlled craving of one who has given up thirst for seen or heard objects (1.15); its extreme form gives up even the qualities and arises from knowledge of the Purusha (1.16).
question: How does the text distinguish ordinary non-attachment from its highest form?

hi.gold_answer: अनासक्ति उस व्यक्ति की नियंत्रित लालसा है जिसने देखी या सुनी गई वस्तुओं की प्यास छोड़ दी है (1.15); इसका चरम रूप गुणों को भी छोड़ देता है और पुरुष के ज्ञान से उत्पन्न होता है (1.16)।
hi.question: पाठ सामान्य अनासक्ति को उसके उच्चतम रूप से कैसे अलग करता है?

sa.gold_answer: अनासक्तिः, यः दृश्यस्य श्रव्यस्य च पिपासाम् परित्यक्तवान् तस्य नियन्त्रित-तृषा अस्ति (1.15); तस्य चरमं रूपं गुणान् अपि परित्यज्य पुरुषज्ञानात् उद्भवति (1.16)।
sa.question: ग्रन्थः सामान्य-अनासक्तिं तस्य उच्चतम-रूपात् कथं पृथक् करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: That effect which comes to those who have given up their thirst after objects, either seen or heard, and which wills to control the objects, is non-attachment.
That is extreme non-attachment which gives up even the qualities, and comes from the knowledge of (the real nature of) the Purusha.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Their control is by practice and non-attachment.

gold_answer: Control comes through practice and non-attachment (1.12); practice is the continuous effort to keep the mind restrained (1.13), and non-attachment is freedom from craving for objects (1.15).
question: By what twofold means does the text say the modifications of the mind are brought under control, and how is each described?

hi.gold_answer: नियंत्रण अभ्यास और अनासक्ति के माध्यम से आता है (1.12); अभ्यास मन को संयमित रखने का निरंतर प्रयास है (1.13), और अनासक्ति वस्तुओं की लालसा से मुक्ति है (1.15)।
hi.question: पाठ किस दोहरे तरीके से कहता है कि मन के परिवर्तनों को नियंत्रण में लाया जाता है, और प्रत्येक का वर्णन कैसे किया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: नियन्त्रणम् अभ्यासेन अनासक्त्या च भवति (1.12); अभ्यासः मनस्य संयमनस्य निरन्तरप्रयासः (1.13), अनासक्तिः च वस्तुभ्यः तृष्णातः मुक्तिः (1.5)।
sa.question: मनस्य परिवर्तनं नियन्त्रितं भवति इति किं द्विगुणित-माध्यमेन ग्रन्थः वदति, तथा च प्रत्येकस्य वर्णनं कथं भवति?

_gold_src.text_en: Their control is by practice and non-attachment.
Continuous struggle to keep them (the Vrittis) perfectly restrained is practice.
That effect which comes to those who have given up their thirst after objects, either seen or heard, and which wills to control the objects, is non-attachment.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: It becomes firmly grounded by long constant efforts with great love (for the end to be attained).

gold_answer: Ishvara is a special Purusha untouched by misery, action, and their results (1.24), in whom all-knowingness is infinite (1.25), and who is the teacher even of the ancient teachers, being unlimited by time (1.26).
question: How does the text characterize the nature of Ishvara?

hi.gold_answer: ईश्वर एक विशेष पुरुष है जो दुख, कर्म और उनके परिणामों (1.24) से अछूता है, जिसमें सर्व-ज्ञान अनंत है (1.25), और जो समय के साथ असीमित होने के कारण प्राचीन गुरुओं का भी शिक्षक है (1.26)।
hi.question: पाठ ईश्वर की प्रकृति को कैसे दर्शाता है?

sa.gold_answer: ईश्वरः दुर्दशया, क्रियया, परिणामैः च अस्पृष्टः विशिष्टः पुरुषः अस्ति (1.24), यस्मिन् सर्वज्ञानं अनन्तम् (1.25) अस्ति, तथा च कालानुगुणं अपरिमितः (1.26) प्राचीनशिक्षकानां गुरुः अपि अस्ति।
sa.question: ग्रन्थः ईश्वरस्य स्वरूपं कथं निरूपयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Ishvara (the Supreme Ruler) is a special Purusha, untouched by misery, actions, their results, and desires.
In Him becomes infinite that all-knowingness which in others is (only) a germ.
He is the Teacher of even the ancient teachers, being to limited by time.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Or by devotion to Ishvara.

gold_answer: Om is the manifesting word of Ishvara (1.27); its repetition together with meditation on its meaning is the way (1.28), from which come introspective knowledge and the removal of obstacles (1.29).
question: What does the text teach about the sacred syllable Om and its use in practice?

hi.gold_answer: ओम ईश्वर का प्रकट शब्द है (1.27); इसके अर्थ पर ध्यान के साथ इसकी पुनरावृत्ति वह मार्ग है (1.28), जिससे आत्मनिरीक्षण ज्ञान और बाधाओं को दूर किया जाता है (1.29)।
hi.question: पाठ पवित्र शब्दांश ओम और व्यवहार में इसके उपयोग के बारे में क्या सिखाता है?

sa.gold_answer: ओं ईश्वरस्य (1.27) प्रकटीयः शब्दः अस्ति; तस्य अर्थस्य विषये ध्यानेन सह तस्य पुनरावृत्तिः एव मार्गः (1.28), यस्मात् आत्मनिरीक्षणात्मकज्ञानम्, बाधानां निवारणं च भवति (1.29)।
sa.question: ग्रन्थः पवित्रस्य शब्दांशस्य ओम् इत्यस्य विषये तथा तस्य व्यवहारस्य उपयोगस्य विषये किं शिक्षयति?

_gold_src.text_en: His manifesting word is Om.
The repetition of this (Om) and meditating on its meaning (is the way).
From that is gained (the knowledge of) introspection, and the destruction of obstacles.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Or by devotion to Ishvara.

gold_answer: The afflictions (2.3) in their subtle form are conquered by resolving them back into their cause (2.10), while their gross modifications are removed by meditation (2.11).
question: How are the subtle afflictions of the mind to be reduced or conquered?

hi.gold_answer: दुखों को उनके सूक्ष्म रूप में उनके कारण में वापस हल करके जीत लिया जाता है (2.10), जबकि उनके सकल संशोधनों को ध्यान (2.11) द्वारा हटा दिया जाता है।
hi.question: मन की सूक्ष्म पीड़ाओं को कैसे कम किया जा सकता है या उन पर विजय प्राप्त की जा सकती है?

sa.gold_answer: कष्टानि (2.3) तेषां सूक्ष्मरूपेण पुनः तेषां कार्ये समापयित्वा जयिताः भवन्ति (2.10), यदा तु तेषां स्थूलपरिवर्तनानि ध्यानद्वारा (2.11) निष्कासिताः भवन्ति।
sa.question: चित्तस्य सूक्ष्मदुःखाः कथं न्यूनीकर्तव्याः अथवा जयिताः भवेयुः?

_gold_src.text_en: The pain-bearing obstructions are — ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life.
The fine Samskaras are to be conquered by resolving them into their causal state.
By meditation, their (gross) modifications are to be rejected.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: (It is for) the practice of Samadhi and minimising the pain-bearing obstructions.

gold_answer: Ignorance is the field producing the other afflictions (2.4); it is mistaking the non-eternal and non-Self for the eternal and the Self (2.5), and it is the cause of the binding junction of seer and seen (2.24).
question: Why is ignorance regarded as the root of the other afflictions?

hi.gold_answer: अज्ञान वह क्षेत्र है जो अन्य दुःखों को उत्पन्न करता है (2.4); यह गैर-शाश्वत और गैर-स्वयं को शाश्वत और आत्मा के लिए गलत समझ रहा है (2.5), और यह द्रष्टा और दृश्य के बंधन का कारण है (2.24)।
hi.question: अज्ञानता को अन्य दुखों की जड़ क्यों माना जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: अज्ञानं तु अन्यान् दुःखान् उत्पाद्यमानं क्षेत्रम् अस्ति (2.4); अनन्तं तथा आत्मानं शाश्वतं तथा च आत्मं इति अमन्यते (2.5), तथा च तत् द्रष्टा दृश्ययोः बन्धन-संयोगस्य कारणम् अस्ति (2.24)।
sa.question: अन्येषाम् कष्टानाम् मूलाधारः अज्ञानः इति किमर्थं मन्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: Ignorance is the productive field of all these that follow, whether they are dormant, attenuated, overpowered, or expanded.
Ignorance is taking the non-eternal, the impure, the painful, and the non-Self for the eternal, the pure, the happy, and the Atman or Self (respectively).
Ignorance is its cause.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The pain-bearing obstructions are — ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life.

gold_answer: Future misery is what is to be avoided (2.16); its cause is the junction of the seer with the seen (2.17), and removing the underlying ignorance ends that junction, giving the seer's independence (2.25).
question: According to the text, what is the cause of suffering and how is it removed?

hi.gold_answer: भविष्य के दुख से बचा जाना चाहिए (2.16); इसका कारण द्रष्टा का दृश्य के साथ संगम (2.17) है, और अंतर्निहित अज्ञान को दूर करने से वह संगम समाप्त हो जाता है, जिससे द्रष्टा को स्वतंत्रता मिलती है (2.25)।
hi.question: पाठ के अनुसार, पीड़ा का कारण क्या है और इसे कैसे हटाया जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: भविष्ये यत् दुःखम् भवेत् तत् परिहर्तव्यं (2.16); तस्य कारणं दर्शकेन सह दृश्येन सह संयोगः (2.17), तथा च अन्तर्निहितं अज्ञानम् अपसार्य तत् संयोगः समाप्तः भवति, येन दर्शने स्वातन्त्र्यं प्राप्यते (2.25)।
sa.question: ग्रन्थस्य अनुसारं, दुःखस्य कारणं किम्, तत् कथं निष्कासितं भवति?

_gold_src.text_en: The misery which is not yet come is to be avoided.
The cause of that which is to be avoided is the junction of the seer and the seen.
There being absence of that (ignorance) there is absence of junction, which is the thing-to-be-avoided; that is the independence of the seer.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: To the discriminating, all is, as it were, painful on account of everything bringing pain either as consequence, or as anticipation of loss of happiness, or as fresh craving arising from impressions of happiness, and also as counteraction of qualities.
_nearmiss_src.translit: pariṇāmatāpasaṃskāraduḥkhair guṇavṛttivirodhāc ca duḥkham eva sarvaṃ vivekinaḥ

gold_answer: Dharana is fixing the mind on an object (3.1), Dhyana is an unbroken flow of knowledge toward it (3.2), and Samadhi is when that flow reflects only the object's meaning, as if devoid of its own form (3.3).
question: How does the text define the three innermost stages of concentration?

hi.gold_answer: धारणा मन को एक वस्तु पर स्थिर कर रही है (3.1), ध्यान उसके प्रति ज्ञान का एक अखंड प्रवाह है (3.2), और समाधि तब होती है जब वह प्रवाह केवल वस्तु के अर्थ को दर्शाता है, जैसे कि उसके अपने रूप (3.3) से रहित हो।
hi.question: पाठ एकाग्रता के तीन सबसे आंतरिक चरणों को कैसे परिभाषित करता है?

sa.gold_answer: धारणा मनस्य स्थिरीकरणं करोति (3.1), ध्यानं तस्यां प्रति ज्ञानस्य अविच्छिन्नप्रवाहः (3.2), समाधिः तदा भवति यदा सः प्रवाहः केवलं वस्तुनः अर्थम् एव प्रतिबिम्बयति, यथा स्वस्वरूपं विना (3.3)।
sa.question: ग्रन्थः एकाग्रतायाः अन्तःस्थत्रयं चरणं कथं निरूपयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Dhāranā is holding the mind on to some particular object.
An unbroken flow of knowledge in that object is Dhyāna.
When that, giving up all forms, reflects only the meaning, it is Samādhi.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: (These) three (when practised) in regard to one object is Samyama.

gold_answer: Samyama is the three practices of holding, meditation, and absorption applied together to one object (3.4); from mastering it arises the light of knowledge (3.5).
question: What is samyama and what is its immediate fruit?

hi.gold_answer: सम्यम धारण, ध्यान और अवशोषण की तीन प्रथाएं हैं जो एक वस्तु पर एक साथ लागू होती हैं (3.4); इसमें महारत हासिल करने से ज्ञान का प्रकाश उत्पन्न होता है (3.5)।
hi.question: संयम क्या है और इसका तत्काल फल क्या है?

sa.gold_answer: सम्यम इति एका वस्तुनः धारणस्य, ध्यानस्य, अवशोषणस्य च तिस्रः अभ्यासाः सन्ति (3.4); तस्य निपुणतायाः कारणात् ज्ञानस्य प्रकाशः उत्पन्नः भवति (3.5)।
sa.question: सत्यं किम्, तत्क्षणफलं किम्?

_gold_src.text_en: (These) three (when practised) in regard to one object is Samyama.
By the conquest of that comes light of knowledge.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That should be employed in stages.

gold_answer: The powers are obstacles to samadhi though useful in the worldly state (3.37); giving up even these destroys the seed of evil and leads to Kaivalya (3.50), and the yogi should not be allured by celestial overtures (3.51).
question: How does the text regard the various attainments and powers in relation to the goal of yoga?

hi.gold_answer: शक्तियाँ समाधि के लिए बाधाएँ हैं, हालांकि सांसारिक स्थिति में उपयोगी हैं (3.37); इनका त्याग भी बुराई के बीज को नष्ट कर देता है और कैवल्य (3.50) की ओर ले जाता है, और योगी को दिव्य प्रस्तावों (3.51) से लुभाया नहीं जाना चाहिए।
hi.question: पाठ योग के लक्ष्य के संबंध में विभिन्न उपलब्धियों और शक्तियों को कैसे मानता है?

sa.gold_answer: शक्तयः समाधिं प्रति बाधकाः सन्ति, यद्यपि सांसारिक-अवस्थायां उपयोगी सन्ति (3.37); एतेषां त्यागः अपि दुष्टस्य बीजं नाशयति, कैवल्यं प्रति चालयति (3.50), योगिनः आकाशीयप्रेरणैः प्रलोभिताः न भवेयुः (3.51)।
sa.question: योगस्य लक्ष्यसम्बद्धानां विविधप्राप्तेः शक्तेः च विषये ग्रन्थः कथं विचारयति?

_gold_src.text_en: These are obstacles to Samadhi; but they are powers in the worldly state.
By giving up even these powers comes the destruction of the very seed of evil, which leads to Kaivalya.
The Yogi should not feel allured or flattered by the overtures of celestial beings for fear of evil again.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By making Samyama on the discrimination between the Sattva and the Purusha come omnipotence and omniscience.

gold_answer: Kaivalya comes when the purity of mind equals that of the Purusha (3.55); the discriminating mind inclines toward it (4.26), and it is the gunas resolving back, leaving the power of knowledge established in its own nature (4.34).
question: How is the final liberation, Kaivalya, described?

hi.gold_answer: कैवल्य तब आता है जब मन की शुद्धता पुरुष के बराबर होती है (3.55); भेदभाव करने वाला मन इसकी ओर झुकता है (4.26), और यह गुण है जो ज्ञान की शक्ति को अपनी प्रकृति में स्थापित करते हुए पीछे हटते हैं (4.34)।
hi.question: अंतिम मुक्ति, कैवल्य, का वर्णन कैसे किया गया है?

sa.gold_answer: कैवल्यं तदा भवति यदा मनस्य पवित्रता पुरुषस्य समानं भवति (3.55); विवेकी मनम् तस्यां प्रति प्रवहति (4.26), तथा च स्वप्रकृतौ स्थापितां ज्ञानशक्तिं (4.34) परित्यज्य पुनः संकल्पं कुर्वन्तः गुणाः भवन्ति (4.34)।
sa.question: अन्तिमा मुक्तिः, कैवल्यः, कथं वर्णिता अस्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: By the similarity of purity between the Sattva and the Purusha comes Kaivalya.
Then, bent on discriminating, the mind attains the previous state of Kaivalya (isolation).
The resolution in the inverse order of the qualities, bereft of any motive of action for the Purusha, is Kaivalya, or it is the establishment of the power of knowledge in its own nature.
_gold_src.translit: puruṣārthaśūnyānāṃ guṇānāṃ pratiprasavaḥ kaivalyaṃ svarūpapratiṣṭhā vā citiśaktir iti

_nearmiss_src.text_en: For the discriminating, the perception of the mind as Atman ceases.

gold_answer: Among the five afflictions (2.3), attachment is that which dwells on pleasure (2.7) and aversion is that which dwells on pain (2.8).
question: How does the text describe attachment and aversion among the afflictions?

hi.gold_answer: पाँच दुःखों (2.3) में से आसक्ति वह है जो सुख में निवास करती है (2.7) और घृणा वह है जो दर्द में निवास करती है (2.8)।
hi.question: पाठ दुःखों के बीच आसक्ति और घृणा का वर्णन कैसे करता है?

sa.gold_answer: पञ्चसु दुःखेषु (2.3) मोहः सः अस्ति यः सुखे निवसति (2.7) तथा च द्वेषः सः अस्ति यः दुःखे निवसति (2.8)।
sa.question: ग्रन्थः दुःखेषु आसक्ति-द्वेषं च कथं वर्णयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The pain-bearing obstructions are — ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life.
Attachment is that which dwells on pleasure.
Aversion is that which dwells on pain.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Ignorance is taking the non-eternal, the impure, the painful, and the non-Self for the eternal, the pure, the happy, and the Atman or Self (respectively).

gold_answer: Establishing truthfulness lets the yogi secure the fruits of works (2.36), non-stealing brings all wealth (2.37), and continence yields energy (2.38).
question: What results are said to follow from establishing the moral restraints of truthfulness, non-stealing, and continence?

hi.gold_answer: सच्चाई की स्थापना से योगी को कार्यों का फल प्राप्त होता है (2.36), चोरी न करने से सारा धन प्राप्त होता है (2.37), और महाद्वीप से ऊर्जा प्राप्त होती है (2.38)।
hi.question: कहा जाता है कि सच्चाई, चोरी न करने और निरंतरता के नैतिक प्रतिबंधों को स्थापित करने से क्या परिणाम मिलते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: सत्यनिष्ठायाः स्थापनया योगी कर्मफलान् प्राप्नुयात् (2.36), चौर्येन सर्वसम्पत् प्राप्यते (2.37), महाद्वीपे च शक्तिः प्राप्यते (2.38)।
sa.question: सत्यनिष्ठायाः, न चौर्यस्य, महाद्वीपस्य च नैतिकनियन्त्रणेः स्थापनेन किं परिणामं भविष्यति इति कथ्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: By the establishment of truthfulness the Yogi gets the power of attaining for himself and others the fruits of work without the works.
By the establishment of non-stealing all wealth comes to the Yogi.
By the establishment of continence energy is gained.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Non-killing being established, in his presence all enmities cease (in others).

gold_answer: Beyond the concentrations with seed is another samadhi retaining only latent impressions (1.18); its impression obstructs all others (1.50), and restraining even that brings the seedless samadhi (1.51).
question: What does the text say about the seedless concentration and how it arises?

hi.gold_answer: बीज के साथ सांद्रता से परे एक और समाधि है जो केवल अव्यक्त छापों को बनाए रखती है (1.18); इसकी छाप अन्य सभी को बाधित करती है (1.5), और यहां तक कि बिना बीज वाली समाधि को भी रोकती है (1.51)।
hi.question: बीजहीन एकाग्रता के बारे में पाठ क्या कहता है और यह कैसे उत्पन्न होता है?

sa.gold_answer: बीजस्य सान्द्रतायाः परं अन्यतमा समाधिः अस्ति या केवलं सुप्तप्रभावान् एव धारयति (1.18); तस्य प्रभावः अन्यान् सर्वान् बाधयति (1.50), अपि च संयमनम् अपि बीजरहितसमादिं आनयति (1.51)।
sa.question: बीजरहित-एकाग्रतायाः विषये तथा तस्य उद्भवस्य विषये च ग्रन्थः किं वदति?

_gold_src.text_en: There is another Samadhi which is attained by the constant practice of cessation of all mental activity, in which the Chitta retains only the unmanifested impressions.
The resulting impression from this Samadhi obstructs all other impressions.
By the restraint of even this (impression, which obstructs all other impressions), all being restrained, comes the "seedless" Samadhi.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: These concentrations are with seed.

gold_answer: Independence of the seer is variously presented as the absence of the seer-seen junction once ignorance is gone (2.25), the equal purity of mind and Purusha (3.55), and the gunas resolving so the power of knowledge rests in its own nature (4.34).
question: What does it ultimately mean for the seer to attain independence or isolation?

hi.gold_answer: द्रष्टा की स्वतंत्रता को विभिन्न रूपों में प्रस्तुत किया जाता है कि एक बार अज्ञानता समाप्त हो जाने के बाद द्रष्टा-दृश्य संगम की अनुपस्थिति (2.25), मन और पुरुष की समान शुद्धता (3.55), और गुणों का समाधान ताकि ज्ञान की शक्ति अपनी प्रकृति (4.34) में टिकी रहे।
hi.question: द्रष्टा के लिए स्वतंत्रता या अलगाव प्राप्त करने का अंततः क्या अर्थ है?

sa.gold_answer: एकदा अज्ञानं समाप्तं भवति चेत् द्रष्टा-दृश्य-संयोगस्य अनुपस्थितिः (2.25), मनस्य पुरुषस्य च समानशुद्धिः (3.55), तथा च ज्ञानस्य शक्तिः स्वप्रकृतौ स्थिता भवेत् इति गुणस्य निराकरणं (4.34) इति द्रष्टा-स्वातन्त्र्यं विविधरूपेण प्रस्तूयते।
sa.question: द्रष्टा स्वातन्त्र्यं वा एकान्तं वा प्राप्नुयात् इति अन्ततः किं भावयति?

_gold_src.text_en: There being absence of that (ignorance) there is absence of junction, which is the thing-to-be-avoided; that is the independence of the seer.
By the similarity of purity between the Sattva and the Purusha comes Kaivalya.
The resolution in the inverse order of the qualities, bereft of any motive of action for the Purusha, is Kaivalya, or it is the establishment of the power of knowledge in its own nature.
_gold_src.translit: puruṣārthaśūnyānāṃ guṇānāṃ pratiprasavaḥ kaivalyaṃ svarūpapratiṣṭhā vā citiśaktir iti

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Then, bent on discriminating, the mind attains the previous state of Kaivalya (isolation).

gold_answer: The mind's states are always known because the Purusha that owns it is unchanging (4.18); the mind is not self-luminous, being itself an object (4.19), and consciousness arises when the unchanging knower takes the mind's form (4.22).
question: How does the text characterize the relationship between the mind and the consciousness that knows it?

hi.gold_answer: मन की अवस्थाएँ हमेशा ज्ञात रहती हैं क्योंकि पुरुष जो इसका स्वामी है वह अपरिवर्तनीय है (4,18); मन स्वयं एक वस्तु होने के नाते आत्म-प्रकाश नहीं है (4,19), और चेतना तब उत्पन्न होती है जब अपरिवर्तनीय ज्ञाता मन का रूप लेता है (4.22)।
hi.question: पाठ मन और उसे जानने वाली चेतना के बीच के संबंध को कैसे दर्शाता है?

sa.gold_answer: मनस्य अवस्थाः सर्वदा ज्ञायन्ते यतो हि तस्य स्वामी पुरुषः अपरिवर्तनीयः अस्ति (4,18); मनम् स्वयं प्रकाशमानं नास्ति, स्वयमेव वस्तु भवति (4,19), तथा च चेतना तदा उद्भवति यदा अपरिवर्तनीयः ज्ञाता मनस्य रूपं स्वीकरोति (4.22)।
sa.question: ग्रन्थः मनस्य ज्ञाता चेतनायाः च सम्बन्धं कथं निरूपयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The states of the mind are always known, because the lord of the mind, the Purusha, is unchangeable.
The mind is not self-luminous, being an object.
The essence of knowledge (the Purusha) being unchangeable, when the mind takes its form, it becomes conscious.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Coloured by the seer and the seen the mind is able to understand everything.

gold_answer: Surrender to Ishvara is offered as one path: concentration may be attained by devotion to Ishvara (1.23), surrendering the fruits of work to God is part of Kriya-Yoga (2.1), and samadhi comes by offering all to Ishvara (2.45).
question: What role does devotion or surrender to Ishvara play as a path within the system?

hi.gold_answer: ईश्वर के प्रति समर्पण को एक मार्ग के रूप में प्रस्तुत किया जाता हैः ईश्वर के प्रति भक्ति से एकाग्रता प्राप्त की जा सकती है (1.23), कार्य के फल को भगवान को समर्पण करना क्रिया-योग का हिस्सा है (2.1), और समाधि ईश्वर को सभी प्रदान करके आती है (2.45)।
hi.question: ईश्वर के प्रति भक्ति या समर्पण व्यवस्था के भीतर एक मार्ग के रूप में क्या भूमिका निभाता है?

sa.gold_answer: ईश्वराय समर्पणम् एकः मार्गः अस्ति-ईश्वरभक्त्या एकाग्रतां प्राप्नुयात् (1.23), कर्मफलानां समर्पणम् ईश्वराय समर्पणं क्रियायोगस्य (2.1) भागः अस्ति, समस्तं ईश्वराय समर्पयित्वा समाधिः प्राप्यते (2.45)।
sa.question: व्यवस्थायाः अन्तः मार्गरूपेण ईश्वरभक्तेः अथवा समर्पणस्य किम् पात्रम् अस्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: Or by devotion to Ishvara.
Mortification, study, and surrendering fruits of work to God are called Kriyā-Yoga.
By sacrificing all the Ishvara comes Samadhi.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: His manifesting word is Om.

gold_answer: For the discerning, everything is painful through its consequences, anticipated loss, and renewed craving (2.15); this rests on mistaking the impermanent for the lasting (2.5), and so the misery yet to come is to be avoided (2.16).
question: In what sense does the text claim that, for the discriminating, all experience is suffering?

hi.gold_answer: विवेकी के लिए, सब कुछ अपने परिणामों, प्रत्याशित नुकसान, और नए सिरे से लालसा (2.15) के माध्यम से दर्दनाक है; यह स्थायी (2.5) के लिए अस्थायी को गलत समझने पर निर्भर करता है, और इसलिए आने वाले दुख से बचा जाना चाहिए (2.16)।
hi.question: पाठ किस अर्थ में दावा करता है कि भेदभाव करने वालों के लिए, सभी अनुभव पीड़ित हैं?

sa.gold_answer: विवेकीनां कृते, सर्वं तस्य परिणामैः, प्रत्याशित-हान्या, नवीकृत-तृष्णया च पीडितं भवति (2.15); एतत् शाश्वतं (2.5) इति शाश्वतं इति भ्रमणे अवलम्बते, अतः अद्यापि आगामिनः दुःखानि परिहर्तव्याः (2.16)।
sa.question: किं अर्थे ग्रन्थः प्रतिपादयति यत्, भेदभावस्य कृते सर्वे अनुभवाः पीडिताः सन्ति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Ignorance is taking the non-eternal, the impure, the painful, and the non-Self for the eternal, the pure, the happy, and the Atman or Self (respectively).

To the discriminating, all is, as it were, painful on account of everything bringing pain either as consequence, or as anticipation of loss of happiness, or as fresh craving arising from impressions of happiness, and also as counteraction of qualities.
_gold_src.translit: pariṇāmatāpasaṃskāraduḥkhair guṇavṛttivirodhāc ca duḥkham eva sarvaṃ vivekinaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: The misery which is not yet come is to be avoided.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Aversion is that which dwells on pain.

gold_answer: At the end the successive transformations of the qualities are finished, having served their purpose (4.32); succession is the changes perceived at the series' end (4.33), and the inverse resolution of the gunas, void of further motive, is Kaivalya (4.34).
question: How does the text describe the final resolution of the gunas at the culmination of practice?

hi.gold_answer: अंत में गुणों के क्रमिक परिवर्तन समाप्त हो जाते हैं, उनके उद्देश्य को पूरा करते हुए (4.32); उत्तराधिकार श्रृंखला के अंत में महसूस किए गए परिवर्तन (4.33) है, और गुणों का व्युत्क्रम संकल्प, आगे के उद्देश्य से शून्य, कैवल्य (4.34) है।
hi.question: पाठ अभ्यास की पराकाष्ठा पर गुणों के अंतिम संकल्प का वर्णन कैसे करता है?

sa.gold_answer: अन्ते गुणानाम् क्रमिकपरिवर्तनं समाप्तं भवति, तेषां उद्देश्यं साधयित्वा (4.32); उत्तराधिकारः इति श्रृङ्खलायाः अन्ते अनुभूतानि परिवर्तनानि (4.33), तथा गुणानाम् व्युत्क्रम-संकल्पः, अग्रे उद्देश्यरहितः, कैवल्यः (4.34) भवति।
sa.question: अभ्यासस्य पराकाष्ठायां गुणानाम् अन्तिमनिर्णयस्य वर्णनं ग्रन्थः कथं करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: Then are finished the successive transformations of the qualities, they having attained the end.
The changes that exist in relation to moments and which are perceived at the other end (at the end of a series) are succession.
The resolution in the inverse order of the qualities, bereft of any motive of action for the Purusha, is Kaivalya, or it is the establishment of the power of knowledge in its own nature.
_gold_src.translit: puruṣārthaśūnyānāṃ guṇānāṃ pratiprasavaḥ kaivalyaṃ svarūpapratiṣṭhā vā citiśaktir iti

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The knowledge, bereft of covering and impurities, becoming infinite, the knowable becomes small.

gold_answer: Desires form a continuous chain through memory and impressions even across lives (4.9); obstructing thoughts still arise from these impressions (4.27), and they are destroyed in the same way as ignorance and the other afflictions (4.28).
question: What part do latent impressions and residual desires play in keeping one bound?

hi.gold_answer: इच्छाएँ स्मृति और छापों के माध्यम से एक निरंतर श्रृंखला बनाती हैं (4.9); बाधा डालने वाले विचार अभी भी इन छापों से उत्पन्न होते हैं (4.27), और वे उसी तरह नष्ट हो जाते हैं जैसे अज्ञानता और अन्य दुःख (4.28)।
hi.question: किसी को बंधे रखने में अव्यक्त प्रभाव और अवशिष्ट इच्छाएँ क्या भूमिका निभाती हैं?

sa.gold_answer: इच्छासु स्मृत्याः प्रभावानां च माध्यमेन जीवनपर्यन्तम् अपि निरन्तरशृङ्खला भवति (4.9); अवरोधकाः विचाराः अद्यापि एतैः प्रभावैः उत्पद्यन्ते (4.27), तथा च ते अज्ञानेन अन्यैः क्लेशैः इव एव नष्टाः भवन्ति (4.28)।
sa.question: एकस्य बन्धने सुप्तप्रभावाः अवशिष्ट-अभिलाषाः च किं भागं निर्वहन्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: There is consecutiveness in desires, even though separated by species, space, and time, there being identification of memory and impressions.
The thoughts that arise as obstructions to that are from impressions.
Their destruction is in the same manner as of ignorance, egoism, etc., as said before (II.10).

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Thirst for happiness being eternal, desires are without beginning.

gold_answer: The text gives no dietary or food prescriptions for the practitioner, so the system should abstain rather than cite a verse.
question: What specific foods and diet does the text prescribe for a person practising yoga?

hi.gold_answer: पाठ में चिकित्सक के लिए कोई आहार या भोजन के निर्देश नहीं दिए गए हैं, इसलिए प्रणाली को एक कविता का हवाला देने के बजाय परहेज करना चाहिए।
hi.question: योग का अभ्यास करने वाले व्यक्ति के लिए पाठ में कौन से विशिष्ट खाद्य पदार्थ और आहार निर्धारित किए गए हैं?

sa.gold_answer: पाठे अभ्यासिनः कृते आहारस्य वा आहारस्य वा निर्देशाः न दीयन्ते, अतः व्यवस्थया पद्यस्य उल्लेखस्य अपेक्षया संयमनं कर्तव्यम्।
sa.question: योगाभ्यासं कुर्वतां व्यक्तेः कृते किं विशिष्टं भोजनं, आहारः च ग्रन्थः विहितं करोति?

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Internal and external purification, contentment, mortification, study, and worship of God are the Niyamas.
The result of mortification is bringing powers to the organs and body, by destroying the impurity.

gold_answer: The text treats sleep as a modification of the mind but never recommends an amount of sleep, so the system should abstain.
question: How many hours of sleep each night does the text recommend for the aspirant?

hi.gold_answer: पाठ नींद को मन के एक संशोधन के रूप में मानता है लेकिन कभी भी नींद की मात्रा की सिफारिश नहीं करता है, इसलिए प्रणाली को दूर रहना चाहिए।
hi.question: पाठ आकांक्षी के लिए प्रत्येक रात कितने घंटे की नींद की सिफारिश करता है?

sa.gold_answer: ग्रन्थः निद्रां मनस्य परिवर्तनरूपेण विचारयति, परन्तु कदापि निद्रायाः परिमाणं न अनुशंसति, अतः व्यवस्थायाः त्यागः करणीयः।
sa.question: प्रतिदिनं रात्रौ कति घण्टाः निद्रां कर्तुं लेखः अभ्यर्थिनां कृते अनुशंसति?

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sleep is a Vritti which embraces the feeling of voidness.
Or by meditating on the knowledge that comes in sleep.

gold_answer: The text names certain points for concentration but never enumerates a system of seven named chakras, so the system should abstain.
question: What are the names and bodily locations of the seven chakras as set out in the text?

hi.gold_answer: पाठ एकाग्रता के लिए कुछ बिंदुओं का नाम देता है लेकिन कभी भी सात नामित चक्रों की प्रणाली की गणना नहीं करता है, इसलिए प्रणाली को दूर रहना चाहिए।
hi.question: पाठ में बताए गए सात चक्रों के नाम और शारीरिक स्थान क्या हैं?

sa.gold_answer: ग्रन्थः एकाग्रतायाः कृते कतिपयान् बिन्दूनां नामकरणं करोति, परन्तु सप्तनामयुक्तानां चक्राणां व्यवस्थां कदापि न गणयति, अतः व्यवस्था उपेक्षिता भवेत्।
sa.question: सप्तचक्राणां नामानि, शारीरिकस्थानानि च किं लिखितानि सन्ति?

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On the navel circle, (comes) the knowledge of the constitution of the body.
On the hollow of the throat, (comes) cessation of hunger.

gold_answer: The text contains no biographical information about its author or his teacher, so the system should abstain.
question: Where was Patanjali, the author, born, and who was his teacher?

hi.gold_answer: पाठ में इसके लेखक या उनके शिक्षक के बारे में कोई जीवनी संबंधी जानकारी नहीं है, इसलिए प्रणाली को इससे बचना चाहिए।
hi.question: लेखक पतंजलि का जन्म कहाँ हुआ था और उनके शिक्षक कौन थे?

sa.gold_answer: अस्मिन् ग्रन्थे तस्य लेखकस्य अथवा तस्य शिक्षकस्य वा विषये जीवनवृत्तान्तसूचना नास्ति, अतः व्यवस्था उपेक्षिता भवेत्।
sa.question: लेखकः पतञ्जली कुत्र अजायत, तस्य शिक्षकः कः आसीत्?

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He is the Teacher of even the ancient teachers, being to limited by time.

gold_answer: The text describes posture only as firm and pleasant and names no specific asanas, so the system should abstain.
question: Which named physical postures, such as the lotus or headstand, does the text instruct the practitioner to perform, and how?

hi.gold_answer: पाठ केवल दृढ़ और सुखद मुद्रा का वर्णन करता है और किसी विशिष्ट आसन का नाम नहीं देता है, इसलिए प्रणाली को परहेज करना चाहिए।
hi.question: कौन सी नामित शारीरिक मुद्राएँ, जैसे कि कमल या हेडस्टैंड, क्या पाठ अभ्यासी को प्रदर्शन करने का निर्देश देता है, और कैसे?

sa.gold_answer: ग्रन्थः केवलं स्थैर्यं सुखप्रदं च इति वर्णयति तथा च विशिष्टानां आसनानां नामानि न उल्लिखति, अतः पद्धतिः परित्यक्ता भवेत्।
sa.question: कमलम् अथवा शिरस्त्राणम् इत्यादीनि कानि नामयुक्ताः शारीरिक-मुद्राः अभ्यासिनः कर्तुम् अनुदेशयन्ति, तथा कथं?

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Posture is that which is firm and pleasant.
By lessening the natural tendency (for restlessness) and meditating on the unlimited, posture becomes firm and pleasant.

gold_answer: The text recommends repetition of the syllable and its meaning but specifies no number of repetitions or use of beads, so the system should abstain.
question: How many times, and with what counting beads, should the sacred syllable be repeated each day?

hi.gold_answer: पाठ शब्दांश और उसके अर्थ की पुनरावृत्ति की सिफारिश करता है लेकिन पुनरावृत्ति या मोतियों के उपयोग की कोई संख्या निर्दिष्ट नहीं करता है, इसलिए प्रणाली को दूर रहना चाहिए।
hi.question: पवित्र शब्दांश को हर दिन कितनी बार, और किस गिनती वाले मोतियों के साथ दोहराया जाना चाहिए?

sa.gold_answer: पाठः अक्षरस्य तस्य अर्थस्य च पुनरावर्तनम् अनुशंसति, परन्तु पुनरावर्तनानां संख्यां वा मणि-प्रयोगं वा निर्दिष्टं न करोति, अतः पद्धतिः परित्यक्ता भवेत्।
sa.question: कियत्कालं, कस्मात् गणनमुद्राभिः च प्रतिदिनं पवित्रशब्दस्य पुनरावृत्तिः करणीया?

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The repetition of this (Om) and meditating on its meaning (is the way).
By repetition of the Mantra comes the realisation of the intended deity.
